sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Dennis, presents with a complaint of heel pain located in the back of his heel and extending up into his calf, particularly after a workout or when he first gets up in the morning.
He has been training for a triathlon, running approximately 10 kilometers, biking for 45-60 minutes, and swimming for 30-60 minutes, five days a week.
He denies stretching before or after his workouts.
The pain is described as tight and tearing, easing up after 10-15 minutes of walking but worsening with prolonged sitting or activity.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's lower extremities were examined, and the Thompson test was negative, indicating no significant pain in the left calf.
The right calf was tender to palpation, particularly in the area of the Achilles tendon.
The patient reported pain with toe extension and heel squeeze.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has Achilles tendinitis due to overuse from aggressive triathlon training.
Problem list: Achilles tendinitis; active
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen or Advil 3 tablets every 6 hours to reduce inflammation.
I ordered 2 physical therapy visits to improve strength and stretching knowledge, particularly in the lower extremities.
The patient is to return in 7-10 days for follow-up after the first physical therapy appointment.
Follow-up: The patient is to return in 7-10 days for follow-up after the first physical therapy appointment.
